text.title: AI Status Digest Pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Status Digest Pipeline application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Apply to a single project per digest. Cross-project rollups require a separate methodology.
Every data source has a typed extraction (velocity int, PR count int, budget pct). Free-form prose = REJECT.
Red-Yellow-Green thresholds (e.g. velocity <70% of plan = R) are declared up front and frozen for the project. Ad-hoc shifts = REJECT.
Digest is owner-signed before send. Unsigned digests = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Auto-sent digests publish errors at scale.

rule.statement: Every section cites a specific source row (ticket id, PR number, budget line). Ungrounded statements = REJECT.
rule.rationale: AI-drafted status hallucinates plausibly; grounding is the only defence.

rule.statement: Each digest carries ISO date + semver. Prior digests append-only for trend.
Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
